CONTENT.Honours: Zenit Saint PetersburgRussian Premier League: 2018–19, 2019–20, 2020–21Russian Cup: 2019–20QarabağAzerbaijan Premier League: 2023–24Azerbaijan Cup: 2023–24
CONTENT.Club career: On 29 July 2015, it was confirmed that Lunyov would be joining FC Ufa on a 1-year contract. He made his Russian Premier League debut for Ufa on 11 September 2016 in a game against FC Krasnodar.On 23 December 2016, Lunyov moved to FC Zenit Saint Petersburg, signing a 4.5-year contract with the club.On 10 July 2021, he signed a two-year contract with Bayer Leverkusen.On 19 August 2023, he signed contract with Qarabağ. On 4 June 2024, Qarabağ announced that Lunyov had left the club after his contract had expired.On 18 June 2024, Lunyov joined Dynamo Moscow on a two-year contract with an optional third year.
CONTENT.External links: Andrey Lunyov at SoccerwayAndrey Lunyov at Sportbox.ru (in Russian)
CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 27 July 2024As of match played 20 November 2020
CONTENT.International career: He made his debut for the Russia national football team on 10 October 2017 in a friendly game against Iran.On 11 May 2018, he was included in Russia's extended 2018 FIFA World Cup squad. On 3 June 2018, he was included in the finalized World Cup squad. He remained on the bench in all the games behind Igor Akinfeev.On 11 May 2021, he was included in the preliminary extended 30-man squad for UEFA Euro 2020. He was not included in the final squad.

SUMMARY: Andrey Yevgenyevich Lunyov (Russian: Андрей Евгеньевич Лунёв, IPA: [ɐnˈdrʲej jɪˈvgʲenʲjɪvʲɪtɕ lʊˈnʲɵf]; born 13 November 1991) is a Russian professional footballer who played as a goalkeeper for Dynamo Moscow and the Russia national team.
